title: Writing - Part 1: Work and Career
description: Your class has been discussing what young people should consider when choosing a career.

content.question: Write an essay discussing two of the factors in your notes. You should explain which factor you think is most important when choosing a career, giving reasons for your opinion.

opinions: Money is ultimately what matters most in a job.
A job you love never feels like work.
Having free time is more valuable than a high salary.

structure.introduction: Introduce the challenge of choosing a career and the two factors you will discuss.
structure.bodyParagraph1: Discuss the first factor (e.g., personal interests) and why it matters.
structure.bodyParagraph2: Discuss the second factor (e.g., job security and salary) and its importance.
structure.conclusion: Evaluate both factors and state which you consider most important.

content.modelAnswer:
Choosing a career is one of the most important decisions a young person will make, and it is one that requires careful thought. With so many options available, it can be difficult to know what to prioritise. This essay will discuss the importance of following personal interests and the practical need for financial security.

Pursuing a career based on personal passion can lead to a much more fulfilling working life. People who genuinely enjoy their work tend to be more motivated, more creative and less likely to experience burnout. When someone is passionate about their job, they are more likely to excel and find lasting satisfaction in what they do.

However, financial considerations are equally important and should not be dismissed. A stable income is necessary to cover basic living costs, support a family and plan for the future. A low salary or job insecurity can cause significant stress, which can ultimately undermine personal happiness regardless of how enjoyable the work is.

In conclusion, while both factors matter, I believe personal fulfilment is the most important consideration. Financial needs can often be met in a variety of ways, but spending the majority of one's waking hours doing something one dislikes can seriously affect overall wellbeing. Ideally, young people should seek careers that offer both passion and reasonable financial reward.
